title: bomarea andimarcana
intro: Bomarea andimarcana, also known as Andina glory lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Alstroemeriaceae. It is native to the Andes mountain range in South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, and treat various infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which may help to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
